reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. Using `q : ℕ` with `q.Prime` matches the standard textbook convention that "prime integer" means positive prime — this is necessary for truth since the statement would fail for negative primes like -5 (where -5 ≡ 3 mod 4 but (-5) = (5) in ℤ[i] with 5 splitting). `hq1 : q ≡ 3 [ZMOD 4]` correctly encodes Int.ModEq for q ≡ 3 (mod 4). `span ({↑q} : Set GaussianInt)` is the ideal (q) in ℤ[i]. `IsField` on the quotient captures "is a field", and `∃ finR : Fintype _, @Fintype.card _ finR = q^2` correctly expresses "has q² elements" (any two Fintype instances yield the same cardinality). The conjunction matches the two-part conclusion of the informal statement.
